title: Shorshe Ilish
instructions: Marinate the fish with turmeric powder and salt.
In a bowl, mix mustard paste, green chilies, mustard oil and salt.
Gently coat the fish pieces with the mustard paste mixture.
In a pan, heat the mustard oil and place the fish pieces.
Cook for 5-7 minutes on each side until the fish is cooked through and the mustard paste is fragrant.
Serve hot with steamed rice.

title: Kosha Mangsho
instructions: Marinate mutton with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, turmeric, and chili powder for at least 2 hours.
Heat mustard oil in a heavy-bottomed pan. Sauté the bay leaves, cinnamon, cardamom, and cloves.
Add sliced onions and fry until golden brown. Add sugar and caramelize.
Add the marinated mutton and potatoes. Cook on high heat for 10-15 minutes.
Lower the heat, cover, and let it slow-cook until the mutton is tender and the oil separates from the gravy. This can take over an hour.
Serve hot with luchi or rice.

title: Chingri Malai Curry
ingredients: 1 large onion, made into a paste
instructions: Marinate the prawns with salt and turmeric.
Heat mustard oil and lightly fry the prawns until they turn pink. Set aside.
In the same oil, add the onion paste and sauté until the raw smell disappears.
Add ginger-garlic paste and green chilies. Fry for another minute.
Stir in the coconut milk, turmeric powder, salt, and a pinch of sugar. Bring to a gentle simmer.
Add the fried prawns and cook for 5-7 minutes until the gravy thickens.
Sprinkle with garam masala powder before serving.
Serve hot with steamed rice.

title: Aloo Posto
ingredients: 1/2 cup poppy seeds (posto), soaked and ground to a paste
instructions: Heat mustard oil in a pan and temper with nigella seeds and slit green chilies.
Add the potato cubes and sauté for 5-7 minutes until they are lightly browned.
Add the poppy seed paste, turmeric powder, and salt. Mix well.
Add half a cup of water, cover, and cook until the potatoes are tender and the gravy has thickened.
Drizzle with a little raw mustard oil before serving.
Best served with rice and dal.

title: Dhokar Dalna
instructions: Grind the soaked chana dal with ginger and green chilies to a coarse paste. Add salt and hing.
Spread the paste on a greased plate and steam for 15 minutes. Let it cool and cut into diamond shapes (dhoka).
Lightly fry the dhokas until golden brown and set aside.
In a pan, heat oil and sauté the cubed potatoes. Add tomato puree and powdered spices.
Add water to make a gravy and bring to a boil. Add salt and sugar.
Gently slide in the fried dhokas and simmer for 5 minutes.
Garnish with ghee and garam masala.

title: Luchi
instructions: In a bowl, mix flour, salt, and ghee until it resembles breadcrumbs.
Gradually add warm water and knead into a soft, pliable dough.
Cover with a damp cloth and rest for 30 minutes.
Divide the dough into small, equal-sized balls.
Roll each ball into a thin circle (puri).
Heat oil in a deep pan. Slide a luchi into the hot oil and press gently with a spatula to help it puff up.
Fry until light golden, then flip and fry the other side.
Serve hot with Aloo'r Dom or Kosha Mangsho.

title: Shukto
instructions: Cut all vegetables into similar-sized pieces.
Fry the bitter gourd slices until crisp and set aside.
Heat ghee in a pan, temper with panch phoron and radhuni.
Add all vegetables (except bitter gourd) and sauté for a few minutes.
Add mustard paste, poppy seed paste, salt, and a pinch of sugar. Mix well.
Add water and cook until vegetables are tender.
Pour in the milk and bring to a simmer. Add the fried bitter gourd.
Cook for another 2 minutes and serve as the first course with rice.

title: Bhetki Macher Paturi
instructions: Make a paste of mustard, coconut, green chilies, turmeric, salt, and mustard oil.
Marinate the fish fillets in this paste for at least 30 minutes.
Lightly roast the banana leaves over an open flame to make them pliable.
Place a marinated fish fillet in the center of each banana leaf square. Fold the leaf to create a neat parcel and tie with a string.
Steam the parcels for 15-20 minutes or pan-fry on low heat until the fish is cooked through.
Serve hot by unwrapping the parcel at the table.

title: Cholar Dal
ingredients: 1/4 cup coconut, thinly sliced or grated
instructions: Boil the soaked dal with turmeric and salt until soft but not mushy.
Heat ghee in a pan and fry the coconut slices until golden brown. Set aside.
In the same ghee, temper with bay leaf, dried red chilies, and cumin seeds.
Add ginger paste and sauté for a minute.
Pour the boiled dal into the pan. Add sugar and salt to balance the slightly sweet taste.
Simmer for 5-7 minutes. Add the fried coconut.
Serve hot with luchi or rice.

title: Begun Bhaja
instructions: Cut the eggplant into thick round slices.
Marinate the slices with salt, turmeric powder, and red chili powder. Add rice flour if using.
Let it rest for 10 minutes.
Heat mustard oil in a flat pan.
Shallow fry the eggplant slices on medium heat until they are golden brown and tender on both sides.
Drain on absorbent paper and serve as a side dish.

title: Muri Ghonto
ingredients: 1 fish head (Rohu or Katla), cut into pieces
instructions: Marinate the fish head pieces with salt and turmeric, then fry until golden brown and set aside.
In the same oil, fry the potato cubes until golden and set aside.
Temper the oil with bay leaf and whole spices. Add chopped onion and fry until translucent.
Add ginger-garlic paste and chopped tomato. Cook until the tomato softens.
Add the soaked rice and all powdered spices. Sauté for a few minutes.
Add the fried fish head and potatoes. Pour in 3-4 cups of hot water.
Cover and cook until the rice and potatoes are done. The consistency should be thick and mushy.
Finish with ghee and garam masala before serving.

title: Potoler Dolma
instructions: Lightly scrape the skin of the pointed gourds, slit one side, and scoop out the seeds.
Sauté chopped onion, ginger-garlic paste, and minced meat/paneer with spices until cooked. This is the filling.
Stuff the hollowed-out gourds with the filling.
In a pan, create a gravy with onion paste, ginger-garlic, yogurt, and spices.
Gently place the stuffed gourds into the gravy.
Cover and cook on low heat for 15-20 minutes, until the gourds are tender and the gravy has thickened.
Serve as a side dish.

title: Kolkata Biryani
instructions: Marinate meat with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, and powdered spices for 2 hours.
Fry the potato halves with a pinch of turmeric until golden and almost cooked. Set aside.
Fry the sliced onions until golden brown (beresta).
Cook the marinated meat until it's 80% done.
Parboil the rice with whole spices until it's 70% cooked.
In a heavy-bottomed pot, layer the cooked meat, potatoes, rice, fried onions, saffron milk, rose water, and ghee.
Seal the pot with a lid and cook on low heat ('dum') for 25-30 minutes.
Gently fluff the biryani before serving.

title: Doi Maach
ingredients: 4-5 pieces of Rohu or Katla fish
instructions: Lightly fry the fish pieces in mustard oil and set aside.
In the same oil, sauté the onion paste until the raw smell is gone.
Add ginger paste and turmeric powder. Cook for a minute.
Lower the heat and slowly add the whisked yogurt, stirring continuously to prevent curdling.
Add salt and a pinch of sugar. Add half a cup of warm water and bring to a simmer.
Gently add the fried fish pieces and slit green chilies.
Cover and cook for 7-10 minutes on low heat.
Sprinkle with garam masala before serving with rice.

title: Labra
ingredients: 2 cups mixed vegetables (pumpkin, potato, eggplant, radish, beans)
instructions: Cut all vegetables into medium-sized chunks.
Heat mustard oil in a large pan. Temper with panch phoron and bay leaves.
Add the vegetables and sauté for 10-12 minutes until they are partially cooked.
Add ginger paste and all powdered spices. Mix well and cook for a few more minutes.
Add salt, a pinch of sugar, and a splash of water. Cover and cook until all vegetables are tender.
Stir in the grated coconut before turning off the heat.
Serve hot as a side dish, often during pujas and festivals.

title: Chicken Rezala
ingredients: 1/2 cup cashew and poppy seed paste
2 large onions, made into a paste
A few drops of kewra water
instructions: Marinate chicken with yogurt, onion paste, ginger-garlic paste, and salt for at least 1 hour.
Heat oil and ghee in a pan. Temper with dried red chilies and a bay leaf.
Add the marinated chicken and cook on high heat for 10 minutes.
Add the cashew-poppy seed paste and white pepper powder. Mix well.
Lower the heat, cover, and cook until the chicken is tender and the gravy is thick and white.
Add a few drops of kewra water at the end for fragrance.
Serve hot with naan or biryani.

title: Mochar Ghonto
instructions: Boil the chopped banana flower with turmeric until tender. Drain and mash lightly.
Heat oil and fry the potato cubes until golden. Set aside.
In the same oil, temper with cumin seeds and bay leaves.
Add ginger paste and sauté. Add cumin powder and turmeric.
Add the boiled banana flower and fried potatoes. Cook for 5-7 minutes.
Stir in the grated coconut, salt, and sugar.
Finish with a spoonful of ghee and garam masala.
Serve with hot rice.
